draft.introduction: The first Servant Song, introducing the mysterious “servant of the Lord” who will bring justice to the nations quietly, without breaking a bruised reed. Matthew 12:18-21 applies it to Jesus.

translation.text: Behold my servant, whom I uphold, my chosen, in whom my soul delights; I have put my Spirit upon him; he will bring forth justice to the nations. He will not cry aloud or lift up his voice in the street; a bruised reed he will not break, and a faintly burning wick he will not quench.
translation.original_text: Ιακωβ ὁ παῖς μου ἀντιλήμψομαι αὐτοῦ Ισραηλ ὁ ἐκλεκτός μου

translator_notes.note: The LXX identifies the servant explicitly as “Jacob...Israel,” a collective reading absent from the Hebrew. This divergence fuels the debate over whether the Servant Songs refer to an individual messiah or to the nation of Israel.
